data.title: Goodbaby International Sets Formal Terms for Remuneration Committee
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Goodbaby International Holdings ( (HK:1086) ) has issued an update.

Goodbaby International Holdings has formalised the terms of reference for its Remuneration Committee, detailing its composition, meeting procedures and governance structure. The committee must comprise a majority of independent non-executive directors, be chaired by an independent non-executive, meet at least annually and maintain full minutes, with authority to hold meetings in person or via electronic means.

The document defines the committee’s core duties, including recommending overall remuneration policy, reviewing and approving management pay proposals and determining or recommending packages for executive directors and senior management. It also tasks the committee with setting policy for executive remuneration, assessing executive performance, approving service contract terms and advising on non-executive directors’ pay, underscoring an effort to strengthen transparency and shareholder accountability in compensation decisions.

More about Goodbaby International Holdings

Goodbaby International Holdings Limited is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong, operating as a group structure referred to as the Company and the Group. It is governed by a board of directors that includes independent non-executive directors, reflecting a typical listed-company corporate governance framework focused on board oversight and committee-led supervision.

data.title: Goodbaby Tightens Governance With Revised Nomination Committee Mandate
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Goodbaby International Holdings ( (HK:1086) ) has shared an announcement.

Goodbaby International Holdings has updated the terms of reference for its Nomination Committee, setting out detailed rules on its composition, appointment, and meeting procedures. The committee must have at least three members, a majority of whom are independent non-executive directors, include at least one director of a different gender, and be chaired by either the board chairman or an independent non-executive director.

The revised mandate formalizes the committee’s responsibilities to review board structure and diversity annually, maintain a board skills matrix, and identify and assess candidates for directorships, including their time commitments and integrity. These changes underscore the group’s emphasis on governance, board effectiveness, and diversity, aiming to strengthen oversight and align the board’s composition with the company’s corporate strategy and shareholder expectations.

More about Goodbaby International Holdings

Goodbaby International Holdings Limited, incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed in Hong Kong under stock code 1086, operates as a corporate group with a formal board and committee governance structure. The company maintains a board comprising executive and independent non-executive directors, reflecting standard governance practices for a listed entity.

data.title: Planetree Director Resigns as Firm Unwinds Warpdrive Profit Guarantee
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An announcement from Planetree International Development Limited ( (HK:0613) ) is now available.

Planetree International Development Limited announced that executive director Dai Bin has resigned effective 21 April 2026 to focus on his personal business, with the board noting there is no disagreement and expressing appreciation for his service. The move marks a leadership change but is presented as orderly and without governance controversy.

The company also disclosed a settlement and termination arrangement related to Dai’s profit guarantee on the acquisition of Warpdrive Technology Limited, under which he will pay a lump sum of HK$15 million to the group and repurchase the target company for HK$1. The settlement is expected to generate a gain of about HK$1.54 million for Planetree, cleanly unwinding profit guarantee obligations and transferring a loss-making, non-operating subsidiary back to Dai, a move the board deems fair and in shareholders’ interests.

More about Planetree International Development Limited

Planetree International Development Limited, incorporated in Bermuda and listed in Hong Kong, operates as an investment and development company. The group pursues acquisitions and disposals of businesses and financial assets to enhance shareholder value, and its portfolio has included technology-related interests such as Warpdrive Technology Limited.

data.title: SiS International plans auditor switch from Deloitte to EY Hong Kong
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

SiS International Holdings ( (HK:0529) ) has provided an announcement.

SiS International Holdings has proposed a change of auditor, with Deloitte Touche Tohmatsu set to retire at the conclusion of the June 2026 annual general meeting and Ernst & Young’s Hong Kong firm to be appointed for the financial year ending 31 December 2026, subject to shareholder approval. The move is intended to align the group’s audit work with its major Thai subsidiary, where EY’s Thailand office already serves as auditor, and is framed as a governance-driven rotation aimed at improving audit efficiency, with the board and Deloitte both stressing there are no disagreements or issues requiring shareholder or creditor attention.

The board and its audit committee evaluated EY Hong Kong under regulatory guidelines, considering independence, industry expertise, audit scope, resource allocation, fees and the benefits of rotating a long-serving auditor, and concluded the appointment is in the interests of the company and its shareholders as a whole. A circular outlining the proposed change and convening the forthcoming annual general meeting will be sent to shareholders in due course, while the company publicly thanked Deloitte for its years of professional service, signaling an orderly transition that should support continuity and confidence among investors and other stakeholders.

More about SiS International Holdings

SiS International Holdings Limited is a Bermuda-incorporated investment holding company listed in Hong Kong, with a group structure that includes SiS Distribution (Thailand) Public Company Limited as an indirectly held, non-wholly owned major subsidiary. The Thai unit is a key contributor to the group’s overall results, giving the company a significant operational and earnings exposure to the Thai market.

data.title: China Vered to Overhaul Articles Ahead of HK Market Reforms
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

China Vered Financial Holding Corporation Limited ( (HK:0245) ) has issued an announcement.

China Vered Financial Holding Corporation Limited plans a comprehensive overhaul of its governance framework, proposing to replace its existing articles of association with a fully amended and restated version. The move, to be put to shareholders at the June 2026 annual general meeting, is aimed at modernising the company’s constitutional documents in line with evolving Hong Kong corporate and listing regulations.

The proposed changes will align the firm’s articles with the updated Companies Ordinance by removing outdated capital concepts such as par value and bearer warrants and simplifying capital alteration procedures. They will also reduce the threshold for demanding a poll at general meetings, update meeting and notice rules, and introduce flexibility for hybrid and virtual shareholder meetings with electronic voting and electronic dissemination of corporate communications.

In anticipation of Hong Kong’s uncertificated securities market initiative, China Vered’s new articles will provide for uncertificated shareholding arrangements and allow corporate action proceeds to be received electronically. The revisions further enable the company to hold repurchased shares as treasury stock and to deal in them, reinforcing capital management options while a detailed circular on the proposed amendments will be sent to shareholders ahead of the vote.

More about China Vered Financial Holding Corporation Limited

China Vered Financial Holding Corporation Limited is a Hong Kong-incorporated financial services group listed on the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong. The company operates across investment and financial holding activities, providing capital markets and related financial services to clients in Hong Kong and, by extension, the broader regional market.

Luk Fook Holdings (International) ( (HK:0590) ) has issued an update.

Luk Fook Holdings reported a strong rebound in retail sales for the fourth quarter ended 31 March 2026, despite facing a very high comparison base from a year earlier. The group’s overall retail sales value rose 19% year-on-year, while retailing revenue surged 36%, driven mainly by higher contributions from gold and platinum products, which expanded to 82% of the sales mix.

Same-store sales climbed 33% in the quarter, with gold products up 42% and fixed-price jewellery up 10%, even as international gold prices in U.S. dollar terms rose more than 70% year-on-year. Hong Kong, Macao and overseas markets outpaced earlier quarters with retail sales value, revenue and same-store sales growing in excess of 39%, underscoring solid demand and supporting the group’s continued shift toward gold investment products.

data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Shanghai Longcheer Technology Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:9611) ) has shared an update.

Shanghai Longcheer Technology has warned that profit attributable to shareholders for the first quarter of 2026 is expected to drop sharply to about RMB15.4 million from RMB154.1 million a year earlier. The decline is mainly attributed to weaker demand in the consumer electronics end market, increased investment in new business areas including AI PCs and automotive electronics, and higher finance costs driven by adverse USD/RMB exchange rate movements.

Management stressed that the figures are based on unaudited internal accounts and could be adjusted when full first-quarter results are released. The profit warning underscores both the pressure from a soft electronics cycle and the upfront costs of the company’s strategic pivot toward AI-enabled computing and automotive electronics, and shareholders and potential investors are urged to exercise caution when trading the stock.

More about Shanghai Longcheer Technology Co., Ltd. Class H

Domaine Power Holdings Limited has again postponed the dispatch of a shareholder circular related to its proposed acquisition of VAX, a major and connected transaction that will be financed in part through the issuance of consideration convertible bonds under a specific mandate. The circular, which will include transaction details, independent board and financial adviser opinions, and the notice of an extraordinary general meeting, is now expected to be sent to shareholders on or before 12 May 2026, reflecting ongoing work to finalise information and extending the timeline for shareholder review and approval.

The delay means investors must wait longer for key documentation needed to assess the VAX deal’s terms, governance safeguards, and potential impact on ownership structure through the convertible bonds. data.description:

  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An announcement from TransThera Sciences (Nanjing). Inc. ( (HK:2617) ) is now available.

TransThera Sciences (Nanjing), Inc. has completed a placing of 5,085,000 new H shares under its general mandate, raising gross proceeds of about HK$290 million at HK$57.03 per share from at least six professional and institutional investors who remain below the substantial shareholder threshold. The new shares represent roughly 1.65% of the enlarged H share capital and 1.26% of total issued shares, modestly diluting existing holders while broadening the investor base.

Net proceeds of approximately HK$282.15 million will be directed mainly to research and development, with about 57% earmarked for advancing clinical programs for its core oncology drug Tinengotinib across multiple cancer indications and for TT-00973. Around 33% of the funds will support manufacturing and commercialization of Tinengotinib in China, including building commercial teams and marketing, while the remaining 10% is allocated to working capital and general corporate purposes, strengthening TransThera’s funding for near- to mid-term growth initiatives.

More about TransThera Sciences (Nanjing). Inc.

TransThera Sciences (Nanjing), Inc. is a biopharmaceutical company based in the People’s Republic of China, focused on developing and commercializing innovative oncology therapies. Its core product, Tinengotinib, targets multiple cancer indications, and the company is also advancing pipeline candidates such as TT-00973 for further clinical development and potential market expansion.

Cabbeen Fashion Limited ( (HK:2030) ) has shared an announcement.

Cabbeen Fashion Limited reported that all nine resolutions put to shareholders at its April 21, 2026 annual general meeting were approved by poll, including the adoption of the 2025 audited accounts and the declaration of a final dividend of HK0.92 cent per share. Shareholders also backed the re-election of directors, the re-appointment of KPMG as auditor and the granting and extension of general mandates for share repurchases and new share issuance.

The voting results, with support levels generally near or above 99%, signal solid shareholder confidence in the current board and governance structure. The renewed capital mandates give the board flexibility for future share buy-backs and equity issuance, which may influence Cabbeen Fashion’s capital management strategy and potential funding options in the year ahead.

More about Cabbeen Fashion Limited

Cabbeen Fashion Limited is a Hong Kong-listed fashion company engaged in the design, manufacture and retail of apparel and related products. The group focuses on the China market, operating under its own branded fashion lines and selling through a mix of self-operated stores and distribution channels targeting mid- to high-end consumers.

data.title: Johnson Electric Flags 21–25% Drop in Annual Profit Despite Stable Sales
data.description:

Johnson Electric Holdings ( (HK:0179) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Johnson Electric has warned that profit attributable to shareholders for the year ended 31 March 2026 is expected to fall to between US$197 million and US$207 million, a drop of roughly 21% to 25% from the prior year, even as total sales and gross margin are projected to remain broadly in line. The board attributed the anticipated earnings decline to higher selling and administrative staff costs, adverse foreign exchange effects on operating expenses, provisions for claims and compensation, and various operational items.

The company said the remainder of the profit deterioration stems largely from a non-cash impairment of intangible assets linked to a past acquisition and an unfavorable swing in the fair value of certain investments, highlighting balance sheet and market-related pressures. While the figures are based on unaudited management accounts and may be adjusted when full-year results are released in late May 2026, the announcement signals a weaker bottom line that may weigh on investor sentiment despite stable revenue and margin performance.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0179) stock is a Buy with a HK$59.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Johnson Electric Holdings stock, see the HK:0179 Stock Forecast page.

More about Johnson Electric Holdings

Johnson Electric Holdings is a Hong Kong–listed manufacturer of motion systems, components and related solutions, serving global industrial and automotive customers. The company is a constituent of the Hang Seng Composite MidCap Index, the Hang Seng Corporate Sustainability Benchmark Index and the S&P EPAC SmallCap Index, underscoring its role in regional equity and sustainability benchmarks.

Its diversified portfolio positions it as a key supplier to global manufacturing value chains, with exposure to cyclical demand in automotive and industrial markets. Inclusion in major indices enhances its visibility among institutional investors and signals ongoing scrutiny of its financial performance and governance standards.
